perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months. The Agreement may be renewed, subject to mutual written approval. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for as long as may be necessary for the purpose of giving effect to each one and every one of them.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months. The Agreement will automatically renew for successive twenty-four (24) month periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for as long as may be necessary for the purpose of giving effect to each one and every one of them.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement may be renewed subject to mutual written approval, implying that both parties must actively agree to the renewal. The modified text introduces an automatic renewal clause unless one party provides written notice, directly contradicting the need for mutual agreement. The contradiction lies in whether renewal requires active agreement or occurs automatically unless actively prevented. If they did not want to renew at all, they would have to send the letter to cancel. In absence of this they will be tied to this agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twenty-four (24) months. The Agreement will automatically renew for successive twenty-four (24) month periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the completion, expiration or termination of this Agreement, the indemnities warranties and undertakings contained or referred to in this Agreement shall continue to subsist for as long as may be necessary for the purpose of giving effect to each one and every one of them.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that the agreement may be renewed subject to 'mutual written approval,' implying an active agreement from both parties is needed for renewal. The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal, contradicting the requirement for mutual agreement. The contradiction arises because the renewal process shifts from requiring active agreement to occurring automatically unless actively prevented. This directly conflicts with the original agreement which stated it required mutual written consent from both parties in order to continue the agreement after the initial term.

perturbation.original_text: SIC understands that TKCI is not acting as a broker-dealer or dealer in securities with respect to any transaction contemplated hereunder, and that any securities which may be sold in respect to any such transaction are being sold directly by or to SIC, to or by TKCI parties to such transaction. SIC acknowledges and agrees that no liability will be attributed to TKCI in connection with the issuance of any equity or securities. Any fees due to TKCI are to be paid solely as finder's fee and as remuneration for other services provided for under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SIC understands that TKCI is acting as a broker-dealer or dealer in securities with respect to any transaction contemplated hereunder, and that any securities which may be sold in respect to any such transaction are being facilitated by TKCI. SIC acknowledges and agrees that no liability will be attributed to TKCI in connection with the issuance of any equity or securities unless TKCI acted with gross negligence or willful misconduct. Any fees due to TKCI are to be paid solely as finder's fee and as remuneration for other services provided for under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states TKCI is *not* acting as a broker-dealer and explicitly disclaims liability. The changed text states TKCI *is* acting as a broker-dealer with securities sold being facilitated by them and introduces an exception to the liability disclaimer (gross negligence or willful misconduct). This directly contradicts the initial statement that TKCI is not a broker-dealer, creating uncertainty about TKCI's role and liability related to securities transactions. This will leave the parties questioning if TKCI can even be responsible if something happens. The exception that was just introduced in this perturbation makes it unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SIC understands that TKCI is acting as a broker-dealer or dealer in securities with respect to any transaction contemplated hereunder, and that any securities which may be sold in respect to any such transaction are being facilitated by TKCI. SIC acknowledges and agrees that no liability will be attributed to TKCI in connection with the issuance of any equity or securities unless TKCI acted with gross negligence or willful misconduct. Any fees due to TKCI are to be paid solely as finder's fee and as remuneration for other services provided for under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly states that TKCI is *not* acting as a broker-dealer and disclaims liability, while the changed text states that TKCI *is* acting as a broker-dealer and introduces an exception to the liability disclaimer (gross negligence or willful misconduct). This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, and as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, creates uncertainty about TKCI's role and liability related to securities transactions. It will leave the parties questioning if TKCI can even be responsible if something happens.
